server.name: cpi-admin-mcp-server
server.description: MCP server for SAP CPI OData APIs

entitySets.description: SAP CPI integration packages (logical containers that group iFlows, value mappings, and other design-time artifacts)
integration flow (iFlow) design-time definitions — the editable integration logic before deployment

navigationProperties.name: Resources
navigationProperties.description: the bundled files (BPMN XML, scripts, schemas, WSDL) inside an iFlow artifact

navigationProperties.name: Configurations
navigationProperties.description: externalized configuration parameters (endpoint URLs, credentials aliases, custom properties) of an iFlow

entitySets.description: deployed (runtime) integration artifacts — check deployment status, version, and errors for running iFlows
value mapping design-time artifacts — lookup tables that translate codes/identifiers between sender and receiver systems

navigationProperties.name: ValMapSchema
navigationProperties.description: the source-to-target mapping schema rows that define how values are translated

entitySets.description: message mapping design-time artifacts — graphical structure-to-structure transformations between source and target message formats
script collection design-time artifacts — reusable Groovy or JavaScript libraries shared across multiple iFlows
custom tag configurations — tenant-level labels used to categorize and filter integration packages
build and deploy task status — track whether an iFlow deployment is queued, running, or finished
message processing logs — execution history for integration flows, used to debug failed messages or monitor processing status

navigationProperties.name: MessageProcessingLogAttachments
navigationProperties.description: payload and header attachments captured during message processing (useful for inspecting actual message content)

navigationProperties.name: ErrorInformations
navigationProperties.description: detailed error information including exception type and stack trace for failed message executions

navigationProperties.name: AdapterAttributes
navigationProperties.description: adapter-specific metadata (e.g. HTTP headers, file names, queue names) recorded by the sender/receiver adapter

navigationProperties.name: CustomHeaderProperties
navigationProperties.description: custom header properties set during iFlow processing via Content Modifier or Script steps

navigationProperties.name: MessageStoreEntries
navigationProperties.description: persisted message store entries linked to this processing log (messages stored via Persist step)

entitySets.description: ID mapping entries — used for exactly-once processing to map source IDs to target IDs and prevent duplicate messages
idempotent repository entries — duplicate-check records that ensure a message is processed only once
data store entries — key-value records persisted by iFlows for cross-message data sharing or temporary storage
integration flow variables — runtime variables persisted between iFlow executions (e.g. timestamps, counters, delta tokens)
number range configurations — auto-incrementing counters used to generate unique sequence numbers in iFlows
message store entries — full messages explicitly persisted via the Persist step for later retrieval or retry
JMS broker instances — the messaging broker(s) provisioned on the tenant, including capacity and queue counts
JMS queue resources — individual message queues with their current depth, capacity, and consumer status
system log files — tenant-level runtime logs (HTTP, default trace, audit) for low-level troubleshooting
archived log files — compressed historical log bundles available for download
keystore entries in the tenant certificate store — SSL/TLS certificates, key pairs, and trusted CA certificates used for secure connections
certificate chain resources — the full X.509 certificate chains associated with keystore entries, useful for verifying trust paths
SSH key resources — public/private key pairs used for SFTP adapter connectivity
user credential artifacts — stored username/password pairs deployed as security material for basic-auth connections
OAuth2 client credential artifacts — client ID/secret pairs and token endpoints for OAuth2 client-credentials-grant connections
secure parameter artifacts — encrypted key-value entries referenced in iFlow externalized parameters to store sensitive configuration values
certificate-to-user mappings — rules that map inbound client certificates to CPI user roles for certificate-based authentication
access policies — fine-grained authorization rules that restrict which user roles can access specific integration artifacts

navigationProperties.name: ArtifactReferences
navigationProperties.description: the specific integration artifacts (iFlows, packages) governed by this access policy

entitySets.description: partner directory entries — trading partners (e.g. customers, suppliers) whose connection details and parameters drive dynamic iFlow routing
partner-specific string parameters — text configuration values (e.g. endpoint URLs, format codes) keyed by partner ID and parameter ID
partner-specific binary parameters — file-based configuration (e.g. XSLT stylesheets, certificates, mapping files) keyed by partner ID and parameter ID
alternative partner entries — additional identifiers (agency/scheme pairs like DUNS, GLN) that map to a primary partner ID for flexible partner lookup
authorized user entries — users permitted to send messages on behalf of a specific partner, used for sender-side authorization checks
